conference.name: ASSC 29 — 29th Annual Meeting of the Association for the Scientific Study of Consciousness
conference.venue: Casa Central, Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile

satellite.name: Neurophenomenology Satellite Meeting
satellite.subtitle: Laying Down a Path While Walking: Thirty Years of Neurophenomenology
satellite.note: ASSC 29 본 학회(6/30–7/3) 직후 열리는 위성 워크숍. 발표자 소속은 프로그램 포스터에 없어 비워 둠 — 현장/사용자 입력으로 채우세요.

sessions.title: Phenomenology for Neurophenomenology: A Practical Guide for Designing, Collecting, Analyzing, and Reporting First-Person Data
Consciousness Science Beyond the Lab: A Practical Guide to Building Thriving Research Communities
Why Rituals Matter: A Cross-Modal Framework for Consciousness Modulation
Testing Theories, Expanding Minds: Lessons Learned and Challenges Ahead
sessions.bio: Dr. Lucia Melloni received her PhD in Psychology from the Universidad Católica in Santiago, having conducted part of her doctoral research at the Max Planck Institute for Brain Research in Frankfurt. She is Principal Investigator and head of the Predictive Brain Department at Ruhr-Universität Bochum, with appointments at NYU Grossman School of Medicine and the CIFAR Brain, Mind & Consciousness Program. Her lab studies the neural underpinnings of perception, consciousness, and memory using intracranial (ECoG, laminar), neuroimaging, and mobile real-world recording methods. She is a leading architect of adversarial collaboration in consciousness science, co-founding the Cogitate Consortium.

sessions.title: Presidential Address — Focusing attention within and between perception and memory
sessions.bio: Kia Nobre is a cognitive neuroscientist at Yale University, where she directs the Center for Neurocognition and Behavior at the Wu Tsai Institute. Her research examines how the brain dynamically and proactively prioritizes and selects information from the sensory stream and from memories to form psychological experience and guide behavior. She obtained her PhD at Yale (1993) and did postdoctoral research at Harvard Medical School. Before returning to Yale (2023) she spent ~30 years at the University of Oxford. She is President of the ASSC.

sessions.title: Welcoming Reception
sessions.note: Please wear your name badge. Drinks and canapés.

sessions.title: The Neurobiological Basis of Consciousness
sessions.bio: James Mac Shine is a Professor of Systems Neuroscience at The University of Sydney. His group maps mechanistic neurobiological models to dynamical network signatures estimated from functional neuroimaging. He completed his PhD at Sydney and postdoctoral work at Stanford, developing approaches for tracking whole-brain network dynamics. He is a joint NHMRC/Bellberry fellow.

sessions.title: Beyond Neural Correlates: Towards a Dynamic Neurophenomenology of Consciousness
sessions.bio: Tristan Bekinschtein is an Anglo-Argentinean biologist (Master in Neurophysiology, PhD in neuroscience, Buenos Aires University). He is a university professor and Turing Fellow at Cambridge, known for his work on variable states of consciousness and auditory feedback. He runs the Consciousness and Cognition Laboratory at Cambridge.

sessions.title: Two non-overlapping probes for psychedelic mechanisms: Low-dose pharmacology and high-ventilation breathwork
sessions.bio: Kim P.C. Kuypers is an Associate Professor at Maastricht University and Head of the Department of Neuropsychology and Psychopharmacology. Her research focuses on the effects of psychedelics and breathwork, using experimental controlled designs and survey-based methods, aiming to advance personalized psychedelic-assisted interventions and mental well-being.

sessions.title: That couldn't have happened: Perceived plausibility in episodic counterfactual thinking
sessions.bio: Felipe De Brigard is Professor in Philosophy, Psychology & Neuroscience and the Center for Cognitive Neuroscience at Duke University, and PI of the Imagination and Modal Cognition Laboratory (IMC Lab). His research focuses on memory and its relations to perception, imagination, attention and consciousness, as well as the foundations of neuroscience and moral psychology.
